senses.definition: to control the movement and the speed of a car, bus, truck, etc. when it is moving

examples.x: You should drive with both hands on the steering wheel.
I usually drive to school, but today I'm taking the bus.
I like to drive along scenic routes to enjoy the countryside.
After a long day at work, he prefers to drive home in silence.
Please be careful and drive within the speed limit.

senses.definition: to use a bicycle to reach one's destination

examples.x: On sunny weekends, families often bike together in the park, enjoying the fresh air and exercise.
To reduce environmental impact, many commuters choose to bike to work instead of driving.
During their vacation, they planned to bike along the scenic trails of the countryside.
As part of the fitness routine, she likes to bike through the neighborhood.
The group of friends decided to bike to the beach, making the journey part of their outdoor adventure.

senses.definition: to sit on open-spaced vehicles like motorcycles or bicycles and be in control of their movements

examples.x: She rides her bike to work every day.
The friends are riding bikes in the park.
Participants in the off-road rally eagerly prepared to ride their dirt bikes through challenging trails in the desert.
On weekends, Sarah loves to ride her mountain bike through the scenic trails in the nearby forest.
John decided to ride his road bike to work, opting for a more eco-friendly and health-conscious commute.

senses.definition: to move or travel using a motor or engine-powered vehicle

examples.x: She motored to work every day in her reliable car.
The boat motored smoothly across the lake.
They motored through the countryside, enjoying the scenic views.
He motored to the airport to catch his flight.
We decided to motor to the beach for the weekend.

senses.definition: to transport or convey goods by truck or a similar vehicle

examples.x: Farmers truck fresh produce to the local market for distribution.
The construction team trucks construction materials to the building site daily.
The furniture company trucks large shipments of sofas and chairs to retailers nationwide.
Local breweries often truck their craft beers to nearby pubs and restaurants.

senses.definition: (of means of transportation) to make a scheduled and regular journey along a specific route

examples.x: Ferries run between the islands year-round.
The shuttle bus runs from the hotel to the airport every hour.
Flights from New York to Los Angeles run frequently.
The express train runs daily from the main station to the airport.

senses.definition: a ride in a vehicle that takes someone from one place to another

examples.x: She gave me a lift to the train station this morning.
We caught a lift with our friend to the concert.
The hotel shuttle provided a convenient lift to the airport.
Can you give me a lift home after the party?
We missed the bus, so we had to thumb a lift from a passing car.

senses.definition: to let a person waiting by a road or street to get inside one's vehicle and give them a ride

examples.x: She stops to pick up strangers stranded in the rain.
I don't usually pick up hitchhikers, but it was pouring rain.
The bus picks up passengers from that stop every hour.

senses.definition: to release or unload people or cargo from a vehicle

examples.x: The bus driver dropped the passengers at the main station.
The delivery person dropped the package at the customer's doorstep.
The shuttle service dropped the hikers at the trailhead early in the morning.
The courier dropped the parcel at the recipient's doorstep with a signature
Parents drop their children at the school gate every morning before heading to work.
Can you drop me near the bank?

senses.definition: to regularly travel to one's place of work and home by different means

examples.x: The introduction of a company shuttle has made it easier for employees to commute.
The office is conveniently located near a subway station, allowing employees to commute efficiently.
Living in the suburbs, they have to commute by car.
The remote work policy has significantly reduced the need for employees to commute daily.
Despite the distance, the flexible work hours allow employees to commute during off-peak times.

senses.definition: to navigate around or avoid something by taking an alternative route or direction
to travel by getting free rides in passing vehicles, typically by standing at the side of the road and signaling drivers to stop

examples.x: During their backpacking adventure, they decided to hitchhike through Europe to experience the local culture more intimately.
In the 1970s, it was common for young travelers to hitchhike across the country, relying on the generosity of strangers for rides.
When their car broke down in the middle of nowhere, they had no choice but to hitchhike to the nearest town for assistance.
After missing the last bus, she had to hitchhike home, hoping to catch a ride from a passing driver.
The backpacker decided to hitchhike to the trailhead instead of waiting for the infrequent bus service.

senses.definition: to get off or out of a vehicle or conveyance, especially after a journey

examples.x: They alighted from the bus at the next stop.
She alighted gracefully from the carriage.
The passengers alighted from the airplane and headed towards the terminal.
He alighted from the taxi and paid the driver.
The tourists alighted from the boat onto the pier.

senses.definition: to get on a means of transportation such as a train, bus, aircraft, ship, etc.

examples.x: Passengers were instructed to board the airplane according to their assigned seat rows.
The conductor announced that it was time to board the train.
The cruise ship's staff guided passengers on how to board the vessel safely.
The tour guide informed the group that it was time to board the tour bus for the city exploration.
The flight attendants asked the passengers to board in an orderly fashion.

senses.definition: to reach and get on a bus, aircraft, or train in time

examples.x: He left his meeting early in order to catch his flight home.
She caught the bus just in time for her morning class.
She usually catches the 8 a.m. metro to the university.
They caught the late-night bus back to their hotel.
They plan to leave the party early to catch the last ferry back home.

senses.definition: to move from a vehicle, airplane, etc. to another in order to continue a journey

examples.x: Passengers will need to change at the next station.
We had to change in Chicago to reach our final destination.
You'll need to change at the bus terminal to continue your journey.
You'll need to change in London to catch your connecting flight.

senses.definition: to use a taxi, bus, train, plane, etc. for transportation
to signal an approaching taxi or bus to stop

examples.x: She hailed a cab in the pouring rain to get home quickly.
He stepped off the curb and hailed the approaching bus.
The man hailed a taxi outside the airport terminal.
We had to walk two blocks before we could successfully hail a cab.
It took him 10 minutes to hail a taxi during rush hour.

senses.definition: to take a short and usually informal trip, often by using public transportation or a small aircraft

examples.x: If you want to avoid traffic, you can always hop the subway to get downtown.
The business executive needed to hop a flight to attend an important meeting in another city.
Instead of driving, they prefer to hop a train when commuting to work.
During the holiday season, many people choose to hop a cruise to tropical destinations.
We can easily hop a taxi to reach the airport on time.

senses.definition: to use a particular route or means of transport in order to go somewhere

examples.x: I usually take the subway to work.
She took a taxi to the concert venue.
She took the bus to the airport for her flight.
Take the bridge across the river to the other side.
Take the second exit after the traffic light.

senses.definition: (of a vehicle) to come to a complete stop at the end of its journey

examples.x: The bus will terminate at the last stop near the train station.
Please stay seated until the train terminates at the final destination.
The ferry terminates at the island's main dock.
The tram terminates at the city center, where many people get off.
The airport shuttle will terminate at Terminal 4.

senses.definition: to physically enter a vehicle, such as a car or taxi

examples.x: She got in the car and drove away.
They decided to get in the car and head to the beach for the day.
The taxi pulled up to the curb, and I quickly got in.
We need to get in the car if we want to make it to the movie on time.
After loading our luggage, we got in the van and started our road trip.

senses.definition: to get off a train

examples.x: Passengers should detrain only when the train comes to a complete stop at the station platform.
Travelers were instructed to detrain quickly and efficiently to allow for a smooth boarding process at the station.
Please wait for the train to fully stop before attempting to detrain for safety reasons.
Passengers detrained as soon as the train came to a complete stop at the station platform.
The elderly passengers were assisted by station staff to safely detrain and navigate the platform.

senses.definition: to travel along a specific path on a regular basis

examples.x: The bus would ply the city streets, stopping at designated locations to pick up and drop off passengers.
Fishermen would ply the river each morning, casting their nets in the hopes of a bountiful catch.
The delivery truck would ply the highway, transporting goods from the warehouse to various retail locations.
As a mail carrier, he would ply his route daily, delivering letters and packages to residents along the way.
In the early hours, the milkman would ply the neighborhood, leaving fresh dairy products at doorsteps.

senses.definition: to take people, goods, etc. from one place to another using a vehicle, ship, or aircraft

examples.x: Every morning, the school bus transports students from their neighborhoods to the school campus.
Cargo planes are designed to transport goods efficiently from one continent to another.
The cruise ship company specializes in transporting passengers to exotic destinations around the world.
The trucking company plays a crucial role in transporting fresh produce from farms to grocery stores.
Public transportation systems in metropolitan areas are essential for transporting large numbers of commuters.

senses.definition: to board a plane or ship

examples.x: After completing the check-in process, the passengers can embark the train to their destination.
Passengers are requested to embark the airplane at Gate 5 for departure.
The captain announced that we could embark the ferry to the nearby island.
The travel agent will provide all the necessary documents required to embark the international flight.

senses.definition: (off passengers) to get off a plane, train, or ship once it has reached its destination
on or into a vehicle such as a bus, train, plane, etc.

examples.x: Everyone was aboard before the plane began taxiing down the runway.
Once the children were safely aboard, the bus pulled away from the curb.

senses.definition: a trip taken by car, typically for leisure or vacation purposes, where the primary mode of transportation is driving on roads and highways

examples.x: They took a road trip across the country to explore new cities.
Our road trip to the mountains was filled with beautiful scenery and great music.
We packed our bags and set off on a spontaneous road trip for the weekend.
The family enjoyed a road trip through the national parks over the summer.
She’s planning a road trip to visit all the historical landmarks in the state.

senses.definition: a journey to a destination and back to the point of departure

examples.x: We booked a round trip to Paris for the weekend.
He was exhausted after the long round trip to the mountains.
I prefer a round trip because it’s more convenient.
She decided to take a round trip by train instead of flying.
The round trip from New York to Boston takes about four hours.
